headline: Bitcoin-native protocol with ~$293.55m TVL; GitHub repo identified but protocol addresses not discovered
short_headline: Nexus BTC discovery incomplete — no on-chain addresses

findings.text: Block-explorer search via site-operators returned no protocol-specific naming tags on Bitcoin blockchain explorers; protocol is tracked as aggregate on DefiLlama but individual addresses not surfaced through standard search paths.
Official deployments doc: btclaw.space identified as documentation site but inaccessible; https://www.bit.com/ (protocol.website) also inaccessible. No deployed-contracts page retrieved.
No audit links recorded in protocol metadata; none discovered through searches.
GitHub repository https://github.com/btcnexus/nexus-protocol confirmed in search results; direct access blocked; no deployment artifacts enumerated from fetched pages.
Protocol.chains=[Bitcoin]; single-chain deployment. Bitcoin block explorer searches (via site: operators) yielded no protocol-tagged addresses.
No factory contracts identified; not applicable for initial discovery pass.
Role taxonomy: no admin_addresses catalogued due to inability to surface specific on-chain addresses.
Admin addresses: zero entries; evidence chain incomplete — discovery blocked by inaccessible documentation and GitHub.

rationale.verdict: Grade unknown because no on-chain Bitcoin addresses, governance entities, multisigs, or admin roles were discovered. The protocol is confirmed to exist (TVL $293.55m on DefiLlama, GitHub repo at https://github.com/btcnexus/nexus-protocol), but both primary documentation sites (bit.com and btclaw.space) and the GitHub repo itself remain inaccessible in this run. Without fetched evidence of deployed contract addresses or governance structure, downstream control/exit/autonomy slices cannot proceed. A follow-up run with working access to btclaw.space, an updated GitHub mirror, or Bitcoin explorer name-tag queries may surface the required addresses.

evidence.shows: NexusBTC is a wrapped Bitcoin token designed for cross-chain DeFi transactions; TVL calculated from BTC wallets in the address list (article snippet).

NEXUS Protocol Bitcoin documentation site with references to https://github.com/btcnexus/nexus-protocol and Taproot (bc1p) address usage; site inaccessible during fetch but URL confirmed via search snippet.
Official GitHub repository for nexus-protocol; git clone and cargo build instructions for Bitcoin node; inaccessible during direct fetch but verified in search results.

unknowns: D1: Bitcoin block-explorer name-tag search (blockchain.com, blockchair.com, other Bitcoin explorers) — no results for 'Nexus BTC' or 'NEXUS Protocol' tags returned.
D2-offchain: Official deployments doc URL not accessible; btclaw.space returned HTTP error; https://www.bit.com/ returned access denied.
D3: No audit links recorded and none discovered; protocol_audit_links is empty.
D4-offchain: GitHub repo https://github.com/btcnexus/nexus-protocol confirmed but direct fetch blocked (permission denied); no broadcast/ or deployments/ artifacts enumerated.
D5: Single Bitcoin chain; no addresses surfaced from any source.
D6: No factory enumeration attempted; no factory addresses identified.
D8: Zero admin_addresses catalogued; unable to trace any address to a fetched URL with a complete evidence chain.

protocol_metadata.about: Nexus BTC is a Bitcoin-native protocol enabling cross-chain transactions within DeFi. With approximately $293.55 million TVL, it uses Taproot addresses for Bitcoin compatibility. The protocol operates a wrapped token model to facilitate asset transfers across chains while maintaining Bitcoin backing.
